title: Title
builder_managed_notice_inactive: This page contains a saved layout variant from an unavailable feature. Review the classic content before publishing.
author: Author
translation_save_first: Save the page first to add its translations.
translation_status_follow_source: This translation publication status follows the source page.
frontend_translation_fallback_notice: This page is not yet available in :requested_locale. Showing the :source_locale version.
confirm_delete_page: Are you sure you want to delete this page?
menu_custom_label_warning: Some menu items use a custom label for this page. Update them to avoid inconsistencies.
system_page_legal_notice_content:
1. Website publisher

Complete this section with the publisher information: company or owner name, legal status, registered address, contact email, phone number, and registration number if applicable.

2. Publication director

State the name of the person responsible for the publication of the website.

3. Hosting provider

Specify the hosting provider name, address, and professional contact details.

4. Intellectual property

Describe the rights that apply to the texts, visuals, logos, videos, and other materials published on the website.

5. Liability

Explain the limits of liability related to the published information and any external links.

6. Contact

Add the contact information to be used for requests related to the website or its content.
system_page_privacy_policy_content:
1. Data controller

Identify the person or organisation responsible for data processing and provide contact details.

2. Data collected

Describe the data collected through forms, newsletters, analytics tools, or business exchanges.

3. Purposes and legal bases

Explain why the data is collected and the legal basis used for each purpose, such as consent, contract performance, or legitimate interest.

4. Retention period

Specify how long each category of data is kept or the criteria used to determine that duration.

5. Recipients

List the service providers, third-party tools, or internal teams that may access the data.

6. Your rights

Describe the rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction, and portability, as well as how to exercise them.

7. Cookies and analytics

Explain how cookies, trackers, and analytics tools are used and how visitor consent is managed.

8. Security

Summarise the reasonable technical and organisational measures used to protect personal data.
system_page_delete_forbidden: This system page is required and cannot be deleted.
system_page_legal_notice_lead: Use this page to provide the mandatory legal details for the website publisher.
system_page_privacy_policy_lead: Explain here how personal data is processed and protected on your website.
confirm_delete_pages_batch: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the selected pages?
pages_tour_list_toolbar_content: Create a new page, filter by status, and open the trash from this toolbar. This is the main entry point for the page lifecycle.
pages_tour_list_batch_content: Select several pages and archive or delete them in bulk. Use this block for cleanup and large updates.
pages_tour_list_table_content: Each row groups the title, available languages, status, and edit access. Translation badges immediately show which variants already exist.
pages_tour_form_translations_content: Work on the source language first, save it, then return to these tabs to open the other locales. The badges immediately show which variants are ready and which still need to be created.
pages_tour_form_editor_content: This area contains the main editing surface for the active page: title, slug, content, and SEO fields for the selected locale.
pages_tour_form_sidebar_content: The right column controls publication status and saving. This is where you validate the version currently being edited.
pages_tour_list_empty_content: Start here by creating a source page in your main language. Once it is saved, you can add translations, complete the SEO, and review the public rendering.
pages_tour_list_ready_next_content: Open an existing page now to update its source language, then move through the translation tabs. Review the public rendering once the content structure is stable.
pages_tour_form_fields_content: Fill in the title, slug, and content for the active language here. In practice, finish the source first, save it, then adapt each translation from that base.
pages_tour_form_status_content: Keep the page in draft while the content and translations are still being validated. Publish only when the source language and SEO are ready for the frontend.
pages_tour_form_seo_content: Complete the SEO title and meta description for the active language here. Do it after the main content so the SEO matches the actual purpose of the page.
pages_tour_form_create_next_content: Save the source page first to create its multilingual group. Then complete the other language tabs and review the public rendering if the layout needs refinement.
pages_tour_form_edit_next_content: Save your changes, review the active language, SEO, and frontend output, then complete any missing translations.
pages_help_intro: Start with a solid source page, then add the needed translations and connect the right page to the site homepage when needed.
pages_help_step_source: Create a complete source page first, with title, slug, content, and SEO.
pages_help_step_translations: Then add the other locales from the translation tabs to keep a clean multilingual group.
pages_help_step_homepage: When the page must become the main entry point, set it as the homepage afterwards.
pages_empty_text: Create a source page first to start the site structure. You can then translate it, complete its SEO, and optionally use it as the homepage.
